articles.title: Storms and rain hit 8 states amid heatwave: Temperatures cross 44°C across Maharashtra and Rajasthan - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Storms and rain hit 8 states amid heatwave:Temperatures cross 44°C across Maharashtra and Rajasthan
Bhopal/Lucknow/Jaipur/Dehradun7 hours ago
On Saturday, rain along with strong winds brought relief from intense heat in many parts of the country.
A total of eight states, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana, Jammu and Kashmir, and Chhattisgarh, saw rainfall with strong winds. Hail was also reported in several places.
In Rajasthan, strong storms affected cities such as Jaipur, Bikaner and Jodhpur. Temperatures dropped below 40°C in 11 cities.
In Jaipur’s Jaisinghpura Khor area, a power line snapped during the storm and fell on the road. A young man riding a bike came into contact with it and died due to electrocution.
In Uttarakhand, hail fell in Dehradun. In Uttarkashi, Chief Minister Pushkar Singh Dhami’s helicopter landed during hail.
However, heatwave conditions continue in several cities. On Saturday, temperatures crossed 44°C in four cities across Maharashtra and Rajasthan.
Wardha in Maharashtra was the hottest at 44.5°C, followed by Chandrapur at 44.4°C. In Rajasthan, Jaisalmer recorded 44.4°C and Barmer 44.3°C.
In Dhamtari, Chhattisgarh, water sprinklers were used on roads to reduce heat.
Weather forecast for the next 2 days
May 4:
Rain is expected in Jammu and Kashmir, Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Chandigarh, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Odisha, Andhra Pradesh and Kerala.
An orange alert for heavy rain has been issued for West Bengal, Assam, Meghalaya and Sikkim.
A heatwave alert remains in West Rajasthan, while Tamil Nadu and Puducherry are expected to see humid conditions.
May 5:
A yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued in Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal and South Karnataka.
Heatwave conditions are expected to continue in Rajasthan, along with alerts for thunderstorms and rain.
Heavy to very heavy rainfall is likely in Sikkim, Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
4 pictures related to the weather across the country
Hail fell in Dehradun, Uttarakhand on Saturday.
In Pushkar, Rajasthan, the wedding procession was taken out under a tent amidst rain.
In Raisen, Madhya Pradesh, the tin roof of a warehouse was blown away in the storm.
In Dhamtari, Chhattisgarh, cold showers were released by installing pipes.
Weather news from the states
Madhya Pradesh: Rain and storms in half of MP, alert in 27 districts including Gwalior-Jabalpur; hail may also fall
Amidst the scorching heat in Madhya Pradesh, a spell of strong winds and rain continues. On Saturday, more than 20 districts, including Bhopal, experienced strong winds and rain at a speed of 30 to 50 km per hour. Hail also fell in many places. According to the Meteorological Department, similar weather will prevail in the state till May 6. There is a rain alert in 27 districts on Sunday.
Rajasthan: One dead due to storm-rain in Jaipur, hundreds of trees fell; Jaisalmer and Phalodi hottest
Rajasthan will also experience storms and rain on Sunday. Strong winds have been blowing since morning in Jaipur and the surrounding areas. Light rain is also occurring with thunder. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for 11 districts. One person died in Jaipur on Saturday due to the storm and rain. Hundreds of trees fell. Houses in many areas were also damaged.
Uttarakhand: Rain likely in all districts today, SDRF rescued 30 tourists stranded in Tehri Lake
There is a possibility of rain in all districts of Uttarakhand on Sunday. There is an alert for snowfall in mountainous regions. On Saturday, Pithoragarh, Uttarkashi, Nainital, Udham Singh Nagar, and Dehradun experienced rain, strong winds, and hailstones. Floating tents were swept away in Tehri Lake due to a sudden storm. Around 25-30 people were trapped in the Dobra-Chanti lake area, who were rescued by the SDRF team.
Haryana: Today, storms and rain in 8 districts including Faridabad, weather will deteriorate across the state from May 4; Hisar is the hottest city
There is an alert for rain and storms in 8 districts of Haryana on Sunday. These include Mahendragarh, Rewari, Gurugram, Nuh, Palwal, Faridabad, Yamunanagar, and Panchkula. According to the Meteorological Department, there may be strong storms and thundershowers with rain across Haryana from May 4 to May 6. Due to this, relief from the heatwave is expected. On Saturday, Hisar was the hottest with 41.1 °C.
Bihar: Storm-rain in 24 districts, winds will blow at a speed of 50km per hour; Weather to normalize after May 5
Rain is expected in 24 districts of Bihar on Sunday. There is also a possibility of lightning with thunder and winds blowing at a speed of 50 kilometres per hour in these districts. During this, people have been advised to remain vigilant. According to the Meteorological Department, the weather will normalise after May 5. Then the scorching sun will emerge, which will increase the temperature and heat.
Punjab: Rain-storm alert in 8 districts, temperatures near normal; relief from heatwave
In Punjab's Pathankot, Gurdaspur, Amritsar, and 8 other districts, there is a possibility of thundershowers and strong winds on Sunday. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature in the state may increase by about 2 °C in the next 48 hours. After that, the temperature will drop by 3 to 4 °C. Rain is expected in most parts of Punjab till May 6. Earlier on Saturday, Bathinda was the hottest city in the state with a maximum temperature of 40.2 °C.

articles.title: Rajasthan Weather: गर्मी से बेहाल राजस्थान के लिए आई गुड न्यूज़, इस दिन से सक्रिय होगा नया पश्चिमी विक्षोभ - NewsTak
articles.search_term: (सूर्याघात OR तापाघात OR "लू लगना" OR "लू से मौत" OR "गर्मी से मौत" OR "हीट स्ट्रोक" OR "सन स्ट्रोक" OR "लू से मरे" OR निर्जलीकरण OR डिहाइड्रेशन OR "अस्पताल में भर्ती" OR "गर्मी से बेहाल" OR "गर्मी से त्राहि-त्राहि") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Weather: गर्मी से बेहाल राजस्थान के लिए आई गुड न्यूज़, इस दिन से सक्रिय होगा नया पश्चिमी विक्षोभ
राजस्थान में भीषण गर्मी का दौर जारी है, जहां बाड़मेर में तापमान 44.6 डिग्री सेल्सियस तक पहुंच गया है, 3 मई को पारा और बढ़ने की आशंका है, जिससे लू (हीटवेव) का प्रकोप बढ़ेगा.
ADVERTISEMENT
Rajasthan Weather: राजस्थान में मई की शुरुआत गर्मी और राहत दोनों का संकेत दे रही है. मौसम विभाग के ताजा बुलेटिन के अनुसार राज्य में एक तरफ तापमान में हल्की बढ़ोतरी का अनुमान है, तो दूसरी ओर पश्चिमी विक्षोभ के सक्रिय होने से कई जिलों में बारिश, आंधी और तेज हवाओं की गतिविधियां देखने को मिल सकती हैं.
